political.s: Zimmer Biomet demonstrates right-leaning political donations with 75% of PAC contributions going to Republicans ($15K out of $20K). 2024 PAC contributions totaled $128,613 with $740,000 in lobbying spending, representing moderate political influence focused on healthcare policy and regulatory compliance.

charity.s: Zimmer Biomet Foundation actively supports diversity, equity and inclusion, STEM education, community health and disaster relief. Company committed $5 million over five years to anti-racism and DEI efforts, with $2 million specifically for NAACP partnership. Foundation provides grants, product donations for medical missions and matches employee charitable contributions.
charity.focus: Diversity/Equity/Inclusion, STEM education, Community health, Disaster relief, Healthcare access

environment.s: Zimmer Biomet has committed to Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) Net-Zero GHG emissions by 2050. 2023 Scope 1 and 2 emissions reduced 7% from prior year. Company operates Zero Waste to Landfill initiative with 18 sites certified. DitchCarbon score of 41/100 indicates moderate environmental performance relative to industry peers.

labor.s: Zimmer Biomet maintains mixed labor practices with strong compliance emphasis and employee wellness programs, but historical manufacturing quality issues at Warsaw facility led to FDA warning letter (resolved Dec 2023). Median employee pay increased 18% to $80,380 in 2025. Company has engaged employee resource groups and union activity remains minimal. Some legal challenges regarding independent contractor arrangements post-acquisition.

dei.s: Zimmer Biomet achieved 100% rating on 2022 Human Rights Campaign Corporate Equality Index and committed $5+ million to anti-racism efforts. Company operates 7+ employee resource groups with 15%+ participation. Targets: 31% women in leadership and 22% people of color in US leadership by 2026. DEI rating stable at 3.8/5 on Glassdoor.

animals.s: Zimmer Biomet uses animal testing in development and testing of orthopedic implants and biologics products, as evidenced by published research involving sheep bone defect models and other animal studies. Company has formal animal care policies and regulatory compliance procedures for medical device development.

guns.s: Not applicable. Zimmer Biomet is a medical device manufacturer specializing in orthopedic reconstructive products, surgical devices and biologics. The company does not manufacture, distribute or sell firearms or gun-related products.

privacy.s: Zimmer Biomet implements compliance programs and privacy policies for patient data and personal information. Company addresses cybersecurity in sustainability reporting and maintains data protection standards. Limited public disclosure of specific privacy practices or incidents suggests mixed privacy governance comparable to healthcare device manufacturers.

execPay.s: CEO Ivan Tornos earned $16.1 million in 2025 (up 7% from $15M in 2024), while median employee pay was $80,380. This creates a CEO-to-median-worker pay ratio of 200:1 (2025). Compensation heavily emphasizes variable/performance-based pay (91.6% for CEO). Shareholders previously approved executive compensation with only 54% support in 2022.
